label: Part 08
text: The farmer looked at the sky, but he could not see the amazing rainbow or the man walking on it. "Here," said the soldier, "kneel down by the side of the road and look up at the sky like I'm doing." "I will if you'll hold my horse," said the farmer, and he jumped down from his horse. As soon as the farmer knelt down, Jack Hannaford jumped onto the horse and escaped! Now, who do you think was more foolish, the farmer or his wife?

sentences.ptbr: de fármer lúkt ét de skái

sentences.ptbr: bât ri kud nót sí di amêizing rêinbou

sentences.en: or the man walking on it.
sentences.pt: ou o homem andando sobre ele.
sentences.ptbr: ór de mén uóking ón it

sentences.en: "kneel down by the side of the road"

sentences.en: "I will if you'll hold my horse," said the farmer,
sentences.pt: "Eu vou se você segurar meu cavalo", disse o fazendeiro,
sentences.ptbr: ái uíl if iúl rôuld mái rórs, séd de fármer

sentences.en: and he jumped down from his horse.
sentences.pt: e ele pulou do seu cavalo.
sentences.ptbr: end ri djâmpt dáun fróm riz rórs

sentences.en: As soon as the farmer knelt down,
sentences.ptbr: és sún és de fármer nélt dáun

sentences.en: Jack Hannaford jumped onto the horse and escaped!
sentences.pt: Jack Hannaford pulou no cavalo e escapou!
sentences.ptbr: djék rénaford djâmpt ântu de rórs end eskêipt

sentences.en: Now, who do you think was more foolish, the farmer or his wife?
sentences.pt: Agora, quem você acha que foi mais tolo, o fazendeiro ou sua esposa?
sentences.ptbr: náu, rú du iú think uóz mór fúlish, de fármer ór riz uáif
